This repository is a small Laravel (v13) application providing an attendance and leave-management API and a SPA frontend.

Quick orientation
- Language: PHP 8.3 (Laravel 13.x) back end, Vite + Vue 3 front end (see `package.json`).
- Single-page frontend served by `routes/web.php` which returns `resources/views/welcome.blade.php` for all routes.
- API controllers live under `app/Http/Controllers/Api` (Auth, User, Attendance, LeaveRequest, Dashboard).
- Eloquent models: `app/Models/User.php`, `AttendanceRecord.php`, `LeaveRequest.php` ŌĆö use these for data shapes and relationships.
- Database migrations in `database/migrations` define schema and important constraints (unique user + date on attendance, handled_by FK on leave_requests).

Developer workflows (commands I observed)
- Install / setup (project root): run Composer then npm, migrate and build assets. The `composer.json` `setup` script encapsulates the steps:

  composer install
  cp .env.example .env (the composer scripts do this automatically)
  php artisan key:generate
  php artisan migrate
  npm install
  npm run build

- Dev mode (concurrently runs server, queue listener, logging, vite dev): use `composer run dev` or replicate the `dev` script in `composer.json`.
- Tests: `composer test` runs `php artisan test` after clearing config cache.

Authentication and API patterns
- Authentication uses Laravel Sanctum. Check `app/Http/Controllers/Api/AuthController.php` for register/login/logout and `createToken(...)` usage.
- API endpoints are implemented as controllers returning JSON directly (no resource classes). Follow existing response shapes (arrays with user + token, paginated results from Eloquent `paginate(20)`).
- Role checks are done with a simple string `role` property on `User` (methods like `isHr()` exist). Many controllers gate behavior by `if ($request->user()->role === 'employee')`.

Data shapes and validation examples (copy these patterns)
- Creating users: see `AuthController::register` validation; password confirmation and `Hash::make` used.
- Leave request validation: `start_date`/`end_date` date rules and `type` enum `sick,vacation,personal,other` (see `LeaveRequestController::store`).
- Attendance status logic: check-in creates or updates `attendance_records` with `status` derived from time (late if after 09:00).

Important project-specific conventions
- Use controllers under `App\Http\Controllers\Api` for JSON APIs. Keep responses as JSON (use `response()->json(...)`).
- Pagination default: `->paginate(20)` across controllers.
- Model casts are specified via a protected `casts()` method (not the older `$casts` property) ŌĆö follow this style when adding casts.
- Attributes: The `User` model uses PHP 8 attributes for fillable/hidden metadata (see `app/Models/User.php`) ŌĆö prefer attributes when adding new models.
- Keep migrations aligned with models: check `database/migrations` for enums and FK behavior (cascadeOnDelete / nullOnDelete).

Where to change behavior (examples)
- Change front-end routing: `routes/web.php` serves the SPA. Modify `resources/views/welcome.blade.php` and `resources/js/*` for UI changes.
- Add API middleware or route grouping: there are no explicit api routes in `routes/api.php` shown here ŌĆö inspect `routes/api.php` when adding new endpoints or middleware.

Debugging tips
- Use `php artisan tinker` to quickly inspect models and relationships.
- To reproduce authentication flows, seed a user via factories (`database/factories/UserFactory.php`) or call the `AuthController::register` endpoint.
- Look at migrations to reproduce DB constraints (use `php artisan migrate:fresh --seed` in local dev).

Examples to reference when implementing changes
- Role-restricted queries: `app/Http/Controllers/Api/AttendanceController::index` ŌĆö employees are limited to their own records.
- Check-in/check-out semantics: `AttendanceController::checkIn` and `checkOut` ŌĆö re-use status logic and unique constraint behavior.
- Leave approval flow: `LeaveRequestController::update` updates `handled_by` and only allows processing when status is `pending`.

What not to change without discussion
- Primary table schemas (migrations) ŌĆö changing enums or FK behavior impacts existing data and tests.
- Authentication mechanism (Sanctum token flows) ŌĆö keep token creation and deletion patterns consistent.

If you need clarification
- Point me to the file you want changed or the API route to extend. If behavior depends on env variables or external services, provide the .env values (do not commit secrets).

This file was generated by analyzing the repository structure and key files. If you'd like more detail (example curl requests, PHPUnit tests, or a short migration plan for a schema change), tell me which area to expand.
